observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer/Property Custodian to renew the PAR every January
of the third year after its issuance or whenever there is a change in the custodianship;
and
b. Henceforth, comply strictly with Section 492 of the GAAM, Volume 1 to ensure the
accountability of the property issued.

We recommended that the ICO-Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to compile the aforementioned documents wherein the signed payrolls will be supported with the SACs, IDs, photographs with names of the recipients, cellphone numbers of the recipients, if any, and other SDs.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-Municipal Treasurer submitted the documents to the Audit Team

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to coordinate with the Barangay official to refund the SAP-ESP granted to her and be acknowledged by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer by issuing an Official Receipt.
observations.managementAction: The Barangay Official refunded the amount granted to her and the Municipal Treasurer provided and refunded the amount to the Source Agency.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended to require the Municipal Assessor, the ICO-Municipal Treasurer, and the Municipal Accountant to henceforth abide with the requirements set forth in RA No. 7160 regarding the procedures relative to setting up of RPT and SET.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-Municipal Treasurer continued to enforce available remedies regarding the collection of taxes and its corresponding penalties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to: a. locate the documents that will establish the mode of acquisition and costs or fair value at the date of acquisition of the unrecorded lots owned by the Municipality or prioritize the creation of the Appraisal Committee for the valuation of the unrecorded lands, titled and untitled, which will be used as the basis of the Municipal Accountant in recording the parcels of land to adjust the Land account; b. facilitate the titling of the cemetery in Barangay Sta. Maria and the 3rd parcel of land of Barangay Pinagkamaligan; and c. make the necessary follow-ups regarding the titling of the market site.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality established the Appraisal Committee and made the accounting entry in the books of accounts to record the land. The Municipality made inquiry regarding the titling of the market site. Actions were made to secure a 3rd owner's copy of the title.
observations.reason: Titling of this parcel of land is still on-going due to the pending issuance of certification of lot status by Land Registration Authority (LRA) for the reason of their noted lot discrepancy to be verified and corrected thru Department of Environment and Natural Resources, Land Management Services under the Regional Technical Director.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. concerned personnel/department heads to make the necessary actions/coordination for the utilization of the unexpended balances; and b. ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to revert the funds due and to refund the unexpended balances to source agencies or consider the utilization of qualified unexpended balances for COVID-19 mitigation projects, subject to the provisions of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality reverted the funds due to the General Funds and refunded the unexpended balances to the source agencies.
observations.reason: Other funds from PhilHealth and the Cash Prize - Red Orchid Award remain unutilized in CY 2021 due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to provide duplicate copy of checks and attach this to the original copy of the DVs being submitted to the Audit Team for post-audit.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-Municipal Treasurer provided and attached the duplicate copy of checks to the original copy of the DV.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all DVs and its SDs are stamped “PAID” in accordance with the above-mentioned Circular.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-Municipal Treasurer ensured that all DVs and its SDs are stamped “PAID”.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, and the Bids and Awards Committee, to: a. continue the implementation of the development projects noted in the CY 2019 AAR as soon as the negotiation is completed; b. observe adequate planning, timing and preparation in the implementation of the development projects to optimally utilize the 20 percent DF to achieve its desired outcomes; and c. henceforth, ensure that the development projects were immediately utilize in the year it was appropriated. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor reprogram the development projects of the purchase of land for the relocation project, to expedite the negotiation process regarding the matter.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality continued the implementation of the development projects noted in the CY 2019 AAR as soon as the negotiation is completed; The Municipality observed adequate planning, timing and preparation in the implementation of the development projects; The Municipality ensured the utilization of the development projects in the year it was appropriated. The Municipality reprogrammed the development projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to review/revisit the planning, budgeting, and other activities done for the capital outlay projects funded out of the said LDRRMF and make appropriate actions in coordination with all concerned departments to ensure its proper utilization/implementation.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO reviewed and made appropriate actions for the utilization of the capital outlay in the MDRRMF in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to: a. ensure that the GAD issues were presented in quantifiable statistical data aligned with the CBMS, GAD database and other sources of SDDs; and b. submit to the Audit Team a copy of the GPB duly endorsed by the DILG and approved by the Municipal Mayor. We also recommended to ensure the implementation of the GAD projects of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person ensured that GAD issues were presented aligned with the CBMS, GAD database and other sources of SDDs. The GAD Focal Person submitted a copy of the GPB to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Some of the GAD PPAs were still not fully-implemented in CY 2021 due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to: a. locate the documents that will establish the mode of acquisition and costs or fair value at the date of acquisition of the unrecorded lots owned by the Municipality or prioritize the creation of the Appraisal Committee for the valuation of the unrecorded lands, titled and untitled, which will be used as the basis of the Municipal Accountant in recording the parcels of land to adjust the Land account balance; b. facilitate the titling of the cemetery in Barangay Sta. Maria and the parcel of land in Barangay Pinagkamaligan; and c. make the necessary follow-ups regarding the titling of the market site.
observations.managementAction: In his letter dated June 16, 2021, the Municipal Assessor cited that they will make their best effort in establishing the mode of acquisition and the costs or fair market value at the date of acquisition of the unrecorded lots owned by the Municipality and if current situation permits, the Municipality will also do their best to facilitate the titling of the untitled lots in favor of the Municipal Government of Calauag. Current status of the tiling of the lots, as well as the documents and actions needed to facilitate/complete the titling process, were also mentioned in the said letter.
observations.reason: Documents that will establish the mode of acquisition costs or fair value at the date of acquisition of the unrecorded lots were still not located, and/or other actions necessary for the recording of the unrecorded lots were still not made in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to compile the aforementioned documents wherein the signed payrolls will be supported with the SACs, IDs, photographs with names of the recipients, cellphone numbers of the recipients, if any, and other SDs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to coordinate with the Barangay official to refund the SAP-ESP granted to her and be acknowledged by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer by issuing an Official Receipt.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue enforcing available remedies regarding the collection of taxes and its corresponding penalties.

We also recommended to require the Municipal Assessor, the ICO-Municipal Treasurer, and the Municipal Accountant to henceforth abide with the requirements set forth in Republic Act No. 7160 regarding the procedures relative to setting-up of RPT and SET.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to:

a. locate the documents that will establish the mode of acquisition and costs or fair value at the date of acquisition of the unrecorded lots owned by the Municipality or prioritize the creation of the Appraisal Committee for the valuation of the unrecorded lands, titled and untitled, which will be used as the basis of the Municipal Accountant in recording the parcels of land to adjust the Land account balance;

b. facilitate the titling of the cemetery in Barangay Sta. Maria and the parcel of land in Barangay Pinagkamaligan; and

c. make the necessary follow-ups regarding the titling of the market site.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. concerned personnel/department heads to make the necessary actions/coordination for the utilization of the aforementioned unexpended balances; and

b. ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to revert the funds due to the General Fund and to refund the unexpended balances to source agencies or consider the utilization of qualified unexpended balances for Corona Virus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) mitigation projects, subject to the provisions of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to provide duplicate copy of checks and attach this to the original copy of the DVs being submitted to the Audit Team for post-audit.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all DVs and its SDs are stamped “PAID” in accordance with the above-mentioned Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the ICO-Municipal Treasurer in the reconciliation of the balances of the CIB account in the TF books as well as the corresponding related asset accounts and to make the necessary adjustments after reconciliation to reflect the correct balances of the respective accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was able to reconcile the CIB balance per bank account in the TF books as well as the balances of the recorded liability accounts in the TF books as well as the balances of the recorded liability accounts corresponding specific accounts to corresponding to the CIB and other asset accounts. Adjustments after reconciliation were already reflected in the financial statements of CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all cash advances, including cash advance for confidential fund, are liquidated at the end of each year.
observations.managementAction: The Cash Advances for Confidential was already liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to submit the details or breakdown of the identified amount of P1,826,157.37. Further, validate the cause of the inclusion of the said amount and make the necessary actions/adjustments relative thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already provided or validated the details of the Other Land Improvements account amounting to P1,826,157.37. Further, validate the cause of the inclusion of the said amount and make the necessary actions/adjustments relative thereto. The Municipal Accountant revalidated the details of the Other Land Improvements account of P1,826,157.37 and already made the necessary adjustments in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Agriculturist to determine the estimated useful life of the infrastructure assets and work animal taking into consideration the factors mentioned in IPSAS 17 so that the Municipal Accountant may be able to compute for the aforementioned assets’ depreciation and record it in the books of accounts for fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The aforementioned assets were already provided the Municipal Accountant in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Agriculturist in determining the fair value less cost to sell of the biological assets and to ensure that these are reported at said value every reporting date.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already made adjustments in the financial statements using the valuation provided by the Municipal Agriculturist to present the Biological Assets at fair value less cost to sell at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue enforcing available remedies regarding the collection of taxes and its corresponding penalties. We also recommended to require the Municipal Assessor, the ICO-Municipal Treasurer, and the Municipal Accountant to henceforth abide with the requirements set forth in RA No. 7160 regarding the procedures relative to setting-up of RPT and SET.
observations.managementAction:
In her letter dated June 17, 2021, the ICO-Municipal Treasurer is amenable with the audit recommendation and informed the Audit Team that they are continuously sending Notice of Delinquency to different barangays. She also stated in her letter that the Certified Lists of Delinquency were posted in accessible areas and Barangay Halls where the properties are located. She also added that a vehicle was purchased in the first quarter of CY 2021 to be used in the conduct of tax campaign in different barangays to reach those in the far-flung areas.
In his letter dated June 16, 2021, the Municipal Assessor stated that the Assessment Roll as of December 2020 was submitted on February 1, 2021. He assured the Audit Team that if hiring of additional personnel will be granted to assist in the preparation of the Assessment Roll, they will make the best effort to carefully observe and adhere with the above-mentioned provision.
observations.reason: The set-up of RPT and SET receivables of CY 2020 was recorded only on December 29, 2020 due to the delayed submission of the Assessment Roll of the Municipal Assessor to the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the clarifications made by the Municipal Accountant with the Municipal Treasurer regarding some changes in the basis of the aforementioned receivables before the set-up in the books of accounts was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. concerned personnel/department heads to make the necessary actions/coordination for the utilization of the aforementioned unexpended balances; and b. ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to revert the funds due to the General Fund and to refund the unexpended balances to source agencies or consider the utilization of qualified unexpended balances for COVID-19 mitigation projects, subject to the provisions of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2.
observations.managementAction:
In her letter dated June 17, 2021, the ICO-Municipal Treasurer mentioned the they will adhere with the recommendations of the Audit Team to utilize the aforementioned unexpended balances and coordinate with the concerned departments to make necessary actions.
In the Summary of Audit Observation and Recommendation (SAOR) submitted to the Audit Team on June 18, 2021, the Municipal Accountant cited that coordination with the concerned departments has already been made regarding the implementation of unexpended balances and the reversion/refund of the unutilized funds to source agencies, whatever is appropriate.
observations.reason: A total of P3,337,546.19 unexpended balances from the Due to NGAs account were neither reverted nor utilized due to non-refund of the balances or non-implementation of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. General Services Officer (GSO) to make the representation to LTO and settle the issue and register the motorcycle.
observations.managementAction: The GSO was able to settle the issue and register the motorcycle in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer and the Bids and Awards Committee, to: a. observe adequate planning, timing and preparation in the implementation of the projects to optimally utilize the 20 percent DF and achieve its desired outcomes; and b. henceforth, ensure that the development projects were immediately utilized in the year it was appropriated.
observations.managementAction: In her letter dated June 21, 2021, the MPDC provided the reasons for the non-implementation of the 20 percent DF in CY 2020. She stated in her letter that the target was not met due to the current COVID-19 pandemic and typhoons that hit the Municipality in the last two quarters of the year. Nonetheless, the MPDC assured that they will continue to implement the development projects and comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Appropriations for eleven projects totaling P24,541,505.20 were still not utilized as of year-end of CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to: a. ensure that the GAD issues were presented in quantifiable statistical data aligned with the CBMS, GAD database and other sources of SDDs; and b. submit to the Audit Team a copy of the GPB duly endorsed by the DILG and approved by the Municipal Mayor.
observations.managementAction: In his letter dated June 17, 2021, the GAD Focal Person stated that they monitored the GAD AR of CY 2020 so that the programs implemented by the concerned offices were accomplished and presented in quantifiable statistical data aligned with their CBMS, GAD database and other sources of SDDs. He also stated in the letter that they will adhere with the recommendations of the Audit Team specifically in the determination of gender issues that exist in the Municipality.
observations.reason: The GPB duly endorsed by the DILG and approved by the Municipal Mayor of CY 2019 was not submitted to the Audit Team in CY 2020 within the prescribed period to facilitate its timely review and verification as well as its comparison with the GAD AR. Also, 53 out of 81 GAD projects listed were not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Human Resource Officer to provide necessary data or vital information in the employees’ GSIS Membership Service Profile database in order to remit the amount due or to refund the erroneous loan payment deductions to employees; b. Municipal Accountant to reclassify the amount to be refunded to the employees to Due to Officers and Employees account; and c. ICO-Municipal Treasurer to consequently remit the amount due to the GSIS as well as to refund the erroneous loan deductions to the employees.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Human Resource Officer facilitated the updating of the employees’ GSIS Membership Service Profile database in order to remit the amount due. The Municipal Accountant reclassified the amount to be refunded to the employees to Due to Officers and Employees account; and processed the disbursement of said fund. The ICO-Municipal Treasurer refunded the erroneous loan payment deductions to the employees through payroll/disbursement voucher with Check No. 1593739 dated August 24, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the certified list of taxpayers at the beginning of the year so as for the Municipal Accountant to timely recognize RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.reason: The set-up for RPT/SET Receivables for CY 2019 was still recorded in December 27, 2019 instead in the beginning of the year due to the late submission of the Assessment Roll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the ICO-Municipal Treasurer in the reconciliation of the balances of the specific accounts to establish the correct CIB balance per bank account in the TF books as well as the corresponding related asset accounts and make the necessary adjustments after reconciliation to reflect the correct balances of the respective accounts in the financial statements.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all cash advances, including cash advance for confidential fund, are liquidated at the end of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to:
a. locate the documents that will establish the mode of acquisition and costs or fair value at the date of acquisition of the unrecorded lots owned by the Municipality or prioritize the creation of the Appraisal Committee for the valuation of the unrecorded lands, titled and untitled, which will be used as the basis of the Municipal Accountant in recording the parcels of land/adjusting the Land account balance;
b. facilitate the titling of the cemetery in Barangay Sta. Maria and the parcel of land in Barangay Pinagkamaligan; and
c. make the necessary follow-ups regarding the titling of the market site.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to submit the details or breakdown of the amount of P1,826,157.37. Further, validate the cause of the inclusion of the said amount and make the necessary actions/adjustments relative thereto.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Agriculturist to determine the estimated useful life of the infrastructure assets and work animal taking into consideration the factors mentioned in IPSAS 17 so that the Municipal Accountant may be able to compute for the aforementioned assets’ depreciation and record it in the books of accounts for fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Agriculturist in determining the fair value less cost to sell of the biological assets and to ensure that these are reported at said value every reporting date.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue enforcing available remedies regarding the collection of taxes and its corresponding penalties; and
b. Municipal Assessor to ensure the timely submission of the assessment roll to the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the preparation of the certified list of taxpayers and the set-up of the RPT and SET receivables by the Municipal Accountant on the first day of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned personnel/department heads to make the necessary actions/coordination regarding the utilization/reversion of the aforementioned unexpended balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the designated General Services Officer to make the necessary representation to LTO and settle the abovementioned issue to facilitate the registration of the motorcycle.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of delinquent RPTs by conducting tax campaign and offering incentives of full payment of tax dues. Exert extra efforts in collecting the delinquent RPTs, impose the remedies provided for and exercise its taxing authority as mandated under the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to have an 11.57 percent collection rate, compared to 10.55 percent in CY 2018. This was supported by an increase in collection of P825,224.00.
observations.reason: The Municipality still needs to continue their efforts in collecting the delinquent RPT/SET Receivables.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the designated GSO to immediately cause registration of the said motor vehicles and to closely monitor its yearly registration in order to avoid payment of penalty for late registration and to protect the Municipality’s interest over its assets.
observations.managementAction: Of the six motor vehicles, three were registered in CY 2019 while two in CY 2020, hence one remains unregistered.
observations.reason: One motor vehicle remains unregistered due to conflict in the date of insurance coverage of the motor vehicle and registration date in the records of LTO.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to follow-up the submission of documents pertaining to the claims of the owners/creditors. If they are unable to support their claims with the necessary documents, revert the account balances to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that a claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has verified the submitted documents of some of the creditors and were eventually paid. The remaining balances were reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF on November 29, 2019 through Kapasiyahan Blg. 79, series of 2019 by the Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to follow-up the submission of documents pertaining to the claims of the owners/creditors and use all available resources to determine the unidentified payee. If no response has been received within the time frame set and the available resources cannot determine the unidentified payee, revert the account balances to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, subject to an authority from the SB through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has verified the submitted documents of some of the creditors and were eventually paid. The remaining balances were reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF on November 29, 2019 through Kapasiyahan Blg. 79, series of 2019 by the Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent that was intended so as to ensure that intended beneficiaries can avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom. Also, instruct the MPDC, in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to observe proper planning and timing so that projects programmed for the year can be fully implemented within the planned year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to have either implement or reprogram a total appropriated amount of P3,200,000.00 from CY 2018. A total appropriated amount of P28,597,103.66 from CY 2019 is continued in CY 2020.
observations.reason: From the projects mentioned in this observation, total appropriated amounts mentioned in this observation remain unimplemented. Moreover, a total appropriated amount of P28,597,103.66 from CY 2019 is continued in CY 2020. Also, the personnel concerned in the implementation of 20 percent Development Fund still need to make the adequate planning, timing and preparation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented. Also, instruct the MPDC in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to observe timeliness/proper planning that projects programmed for the year are implemented.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive convened the Local Development Council (LDC) and conducted a workshop to objectively prioritize which programs/projects are viable and worthy of continuation and reprogrammed.
observations.reason: For CY 2018, some PPAs of the 20 percent Development Fund were implemented and completed, some are for implementation in the ensuing year and others were reprogrammed. Reiterated in Part II - B. 20 percent Development Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-Municipal
Treasurer to enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes by conducting tax
campaign and offering incentives of full payment of tax dues. Exert extra efforts in
collecting the delinquent real property taxes, impose the remedies provided for, and
exercise its taxing authority as mandated under the Local Government Code.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit
the certified list of taxpayers at the beginning of the year so as for the Municipal
Accountant to timely recognize RPT and SET Receivables.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the designated GSO to
immediately cause the registration of the said motor vehicles and to closely
monitor its yearly registration in order to avoid payment of penalty for late
registration and to protect the Municipality’s interest over its assets.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Accountant to require the submission of documents pertaining to the
claims of the account owners/creditors. If they are unable to support their claims
with the necessary documents, revert the account balances to the Unappropriated
Surplus of the GF, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB)
through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event
that a claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No.
1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to (a) follow-up the submission of documents pertaining to the claims
of the account owners/creditors and (b) use all available resources to determine the
unidentified payee. If no response has been received within the time frame set and
the available resources cannot determine the unidentified payee, revert the
account balances to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, subject to an authority
from the SB through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made
in the event that claim arises in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records on cash accounts at least every end of the quarter to ensure the reliability of cash account balances.
observations.managementAction: An employee hired under job order was assigned to update the Municipal cashbook.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes by conducting tax campaign and offering incentives of full payment of tax dues. Exert extra efforts in collecting the delinquent real property taxes, impose the remedies provided for, and exercise its taxing authority as mandated under the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality continuously sent notice of delinquency to delinquent taxpayers and posted the list in Barangay Halls. They have also submitted 100 delinquent taxpayers to the Province for auction.
observations.reason: Lack of rigorous tax campaign by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. Reiterated in Part II. Observation No. 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to confirm with the account owners/creditors/employees and to request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims to the Municipality. In the event that they fail to support their claims, revert the accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that a claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the Bids and Awards mailing Committee, sent confirmation letters to account owners/creditors whose mailing addresses were known which includes requests for submission of pertinent documents to support their claims. Some account owners/creditors were able to provide the complete supporting documents for their claims, thus were the only accounts processed for payment.
observations.reason: There were account owners/creditors whose mailing addresses were unavailable and a significant number of account owners/creditors have not yet responded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to analyze the accounts, confirm with account owners/creditors the existence of their claims and process payment if proper, otherwise revert the accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stated that they are already in the process of preparing the documentations needed for the Sangguniang Bayan’s Resolution to revert the account balances to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF particularly on the balances of account owners/creditors who has denied any claim or has not responded to the confirmation letter sent by the Municipality. Furthermore, the SB concurred on the recommendation to revert the balances to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF and shall process the Resolution as soon as the documents are formally submitted to their office.
observations.reason: There were account owners/creditors whose mailing addresses were unavailable and a significant number of account owners/creditors have not yet responded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the transfer of the amount of Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable from General Fund to Trust Fund book.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant implemented transferred the amount representing Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable from General Fund to Trust Fund Book. The remaining balance retained in the General Fund book pursuant to Section 94 of the MNGAS for LGU, Vol. I represents retention fees pertaining to transactions/projects under the General Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal
Mayor:

»

utilize fully the 20% Development
Fund to ensure that the intended
beneficiaries can  avail the
maximum benefits that may be
derived by observing the timeliness
of activities involved in the
procurement process thru proper
planning to ensure that projects
programmed for the year are
implemented; and

instruct the MPDC in coordination
with the Office of the Municipal
Engineer to fast tract the
implementation of all
infrastructure  projects  funded
from the 20% Development Fund,
as well as projects funded by
National Government Agencies for
the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality
convened the Local
Development Council
(LDC) and conducted
a workshop to
objectively  prioritize
programs/projects that
are still viable and
worthy of
implementation  and
constructively classify
which will be
continued or
reprogrammed.

The body also
discussed that funds
for some projects
which were sourced
out from the national
agencies are to be
implemented this CY
2018 and the
augmentation fund
provided to Barangays
will be implemented
by the Municipal
Engineering Office.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

For CY 2017, PPAs of the
20% Development Fund are implemented and completed, some of the
projects are for Continuing Appropriations awaiting the CSHP certification
from DOLE, major projects are temporarily held in abeyance due to
unworkable condition in the worksite and road going to the area is
inaccessible to heavy equipment, and other projects for the Barangays are
reprogrammed in 2018. Reiterated in Part I Observation No. 3.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records on cash accounts
at least every end of the quarter to ensure the reliability of cash account balances.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
1CO-Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes
by conducting tax campaign and offering incentives of full payment of tax dues,
Exert extra efforts in collecting the delinquent real property taxes, impose the
remedies provided for, and exercise its taxing authority as mandated under the
Local Government Code.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to maximize the
utilization of the 20% Development Fund so that all its programmed development
projects could be implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries
avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom. Also, instruct the
MPDC in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to observe
timeliness/proper planning that projects programmed for the year are
implemented.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to confirm with the account owners/creditors/employees and to
request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims to the Municipality.
In the event that they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the
Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation
shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions
of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to analyze the accounts, confirm with account owners the existence of
their claims from the Municipal Government, process payment if proper,
otherwise, revert the accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General
Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the transfer of the
amount of $2,524,768.63 representing Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable
account from General Fund to Trust Fund book pursuant to Section 94 of the
MNGAS for LGU, Vol. L.

We reiterated our recommendation that
the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-
Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort
and adopt remedies to enforce collection
of delinquent real property taxes in
accordance with R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality
continued to  send
notice of delinquency
letters through regular
mail and posting in
public  places and
Barangays Halls. They
also conducted tax
campaign in every
“Ugnayan sa
Barangay™ and
informed the taxpayers
regarding the availment
of
compromise
agreement.
observations.reason:
Lack of rigorous and
extensive tax campaign by
the Municipal Treasurer’s
Office. Reiterated in Part
II. Observation No. 2.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal
Mayor hire the qualified physician for
the position of Chief of Hospital, a
regular plantilla position as well as the
required workforce for the Infirmary
with the assistance of the DOH in
accordance with CSC Rules and
Regulations and refrain from hiring
Medical Officer employed by other
Municipality/DOH-Office of  the
Secretary while adhering strictly to the
provisions of R.A. No. 7305 or the
Revised IRR on the Magna Carta of
Public Health Workers.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality
already hired a Chief
of  Hospital.  The
hospital is also assisted
with Manpower
Services by the DOH
such as  Medical
specialist I,  four
nurses through the
Nurse Deployment
Program (NDP) and
three midwives under
the Rural  Health
Midwives Placement
Program  (RHMPP).
Also, the hospital has
the support from
neighboring
municipalities through
the Inter-Local Health
Zone-GLOBUCAT for
additional  manpower
in order for the Health
Facility to function
24/7.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal
Mayor require the Municipal Budget
Officer to appropriate at least 1% of
their IRA for LCPC as required under
DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-
120.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality
adhered to the DILG
Memorandum Circular
No. 2012-120 and
already has an
appropriation of 1% of
the IRA for the Local
Council for  the
Protection of Children.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality
require the concerned Barangays to
submit Liquidation Report relative to the
utilization of the augmentation of funds
to serve as a verifiable basis for
monitoring and audit. The withholding
of succeeding/ subsequent releases
pending submission of Liquidation
Reports may be resorted to enforce
compliance.

We also recommended that the
Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Engineer to monitor/conduct validation
of the actual accomplishments not only
after the Certificate of Completion is
submitted by the Barangays but even
during the implementation phase in
order to ensure that the purpose(s) for
which said assistance was released were
fully achieved on time.

Proper planning of the programmed
projects for the Barangays out of the
20% Municipal Development Fund
should be enforced to achieve fully the
desired results which will benefit its
constituents.
observations.managementAction:
Liquidation ~ Reports
are already submitted
by the Barangays.
Augmentation of funds
to Barangays are no
longer
transferred/released to
the Barangays. It is
now the Municipality
that implements
projects for  the
Barangays.

Implementation  and
accomplishment of the
projects are under the
supervision and
monitoring  of  the
Municipal Engineering
Office.

Due to erratic weather
condition in their area,
the LGU consider all
kinds of constraints if
the  projects  are
doable/feasible to be
accomplished for the
year.
observations.reason:
Due to erratic weather
condition in their area,
the LGU consider all
kinds of constraints if
the projects are
doable/feasible to be
accomplished for the
year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal
Mayor as Head of Procuring Entity and
the Bids and Award Committee (BAC)
1o adhere strictly to the provision of RA
9184 by issuing Notice to Proceed
within two calendar days from the date
the contract agreement has been
executed for the purpose. Likewise,
instruct the Municipal Engineer to
monitor the implementation of the
project and require the contractor to start
the project after the allowable seven
calendar days after the receipt of Notice
to Proceed.
observations.managementAction:
The HOPE and BAC
are now properly and
carefully  observing
and adhering strictly
on the provision of RA
No. 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal
Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer
to compute the corresponding liquidated
damages on the number of days delay
in the implementation on the seven
projects in accordance with Section 68
of RA No. 9184 and inform the
contractor of his liability.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor
and the Municipal
Engineer justified the
cause of delay of
projects were due to
unworkable site
condition, unpassable
road section for the
delivery of
construction materials,
heavy equipment and
heavy dump trucks
loaded with Aggregate
Base Course materials
that may pass by that
may affect/damage the
road  section and
unfavorable  weather
condition, thus the
Municipality ~ granted
the contractor’s
request for  time
suspension.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Special Disbursing Officer to refund the amount of P309,000.00 representing the overdrawn Cash Advance for Confidential Fund and ensure that supporting documents such as the obligation request are properly filled up as to the necessary data or information.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality sent a letter of appeal to the COA-Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Unit (ICFAU) for reconsideration.
observations.reason: The Municipality is awaiting for the result of appeal from ICFAU.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort and adopt remedies to enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes in accordance with R.A.  No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor hire  the qualified physician for the position of Chief of  Hospital, a regular plantilla position as  well as the required workforce for the Infirmary with the assistance  of the DOH in accordance with  CSC Rules and Regulations and  refrain from  hiring Medical Officer employed by other Municipality/DOH-Office of the Secretary while adhering strictly to the provisions of  R.A. No. 7305 or the Revised IRR on the Magna Carta of Public Health Workers.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for LCPC as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
utilize fully the 20% Development Fund to ensure that the intended beneficiaries can avail the maximum benefits that may be derived by observing the timeliness of activities involved in the procurement process thru proper planning to ensure that projects programmed for the year are implemented; and
instruct the MPDC in coordination with the  Office of the Municipal Engineer to fast tract the implementation of all infrastructure projects funded  from the 20% Development Fund, as well as projects  funded by National Government Agencies for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended that the Municipality require the concerned Barangays to submit Liquidation Report relative to the utilization of the augmentation of funds to serve as a verifiable basis for monitoring and audit.  The withholding of succeeding/ subsequent releases pending submission of Liquidation Reports may be resorted to enforce compliance.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to monitor/conduct validation of the actual accomplishments not only after the Certificate of Completion is submitted by the Barangays but even during the implementation phase in order to ensure that the purpose(s) for which said assistance was released were fully achieved on time.

Proper planning of the programmed projects for the Barangays out of the 20% Municipal Development Fund should be enforced to achieve fully the desired results which will benefit its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as Head of Procuring Entity and the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to adhere strictly to the provision of RA 9184 by issuing Notice to Proceed within two calendar days from the date the contract agreement has been executed  for the purpose.  Likewise, instruct the Municipal Engineer to monitor the implementation of the project and require the contractor to start the project after the allowable seven calendar days after the receipt of Notice to Proceed.

We recommended that the  Municipal Mayor  instruct the Municipal Engineer  to compute the corresponding liquidated damages  on  the number of days  delay in the implementation on the seven  projects in accordance with Section 68 of  RA No. 9184 and  inform the  contractor  of  his liability.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit promptly and intact all moneys received to the authorized depository bank of the Municipality in compliance with Section 32, Chapter 3, Volume I of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) to avoid loss and misuse of funds.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-Municipal Treasurer deposits the collections regularly,  although not on a daily basis due to availability of the service car.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to prepare and submit the IIRUP and undertake the necessary action for the disposal of the said properties in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-Municipal Treasurer submitted the IIRUP and scheduled for disposal.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort and adopt remedies to enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes in accordance with RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office continuously sent notices of tax delinquencies through their concerned barangays. Also, conducted dissemination of  how to avail  of compromise agreement offered by the Provincial Treasurer’s Office.
observations.reason: Collection letters received by the delinquent taxpayers were not acted upon due to financial constraints.  Reiterated in Part II.  Observation No. 1

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to submit required data to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period with the end view of generating timely and relevant audit results.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC already prepared and submitted quarterly the required report to the Auditor’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board Officials strictly observe the provisions of DepEd Order No. 12, series of 2014, Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and other rules and regulations, relating to the defined usage or utilization of the SEF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality complied fully with the provisions on the utilization of SEF.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Council to fully utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes which provide benefits to its intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and Engineering Offices supervised the implementation of PPAs of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Some of the projects were implemented in CY 2016, others were reprogrammed. Projects for various Barangays were not implemented due to unworkable condition, other PPAs like public building projects were shelved temporarily due to absence of proof of donation or ownership of the land by the Barangays. Reiterated in Part II. Observation No. 4
